title: ZenRows Universal Scraper API Request
description: Structural documentation of the query parameters accepted by GET https://api.zenrows.com/v1/. Used to drive request builders, dashboards, and Naftiko capability inputs.

mode.description: Set to auto to enable Adaptive Stealth Mode anti-bot bypass.

js_render.description: Render the page in a real browser before returning content.

premium_proxy.description: Route the request through the residential proxy pool.

proxy_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for proxy geolocation.

custom_headers.description: Forward custom HTTP headers from the request to the target site.

session_id.description: Pin the same proxy IP across requests for up to 10 minutes.

js_instructions.description: Base64-encoded list of browser instructions to execute after page load.

wait.description: Fixed delay in milliseconds after page load before returning the response.

wait_for.description: CSS selector to wait for before returning the response.

block_resources.description: Comma-separated resource types to block during rendering (image, stylesheet, font, media, etc.).

css_extractor.description: URL-encoded JSON object mapping field names to CSS selectors for structured extraction.

autoparse.description: Automatically parse the page into structured JSON via ZenRows' built-in extractors.

json_response.description: Return the response wrapped in JSON, including network requests and metadata.

response_type.description: Convert the rendered response into another format.

screenshot.description: Capture an above-the-fold screenshot of the page.

screenshot_fullpage.description: Capture a full-page screenshot of the page.

screenshot_selector.description: CSS selector to scope the screenshot to a single element.

outputs.description: Comma-separated structured outputs to extract (e.g. emails,phone_numbers,links,images).

original_status.description: Return the target site's original HTTP status code rather than ZenRows' normalized code.

allowed_status_codes.description: Comma-separated HTTP status codes for which content should still be returned.
